candidate_llm_application: CONDOLENCE-PROTOCOL: multi-agent state-recovery via fixed 3-stage atomic ritual (invalidate-cache / reload-role / restore-channel) + shared role-anchor cane registry + two-moiety peer execution

independent_scoring.rationale: Formal repair of coordination protocols (races/handshakes/premature termination) directly subsumes the atomic 3-stage recovery dimension.

independent_scoring.rationale: MAST taxonomy explicitly enumerates role-anchor + handshake failures targeted by CONDOLENCE.

independent_scoring.rationale: Production multi-agent recovery with role re-anchoring is a direct functional twin.

independent_scoring.rationale: Semantic role-anchor admission per autonomous peer functionally matches shared role-anchor cane registry.

independent_scoring.rationale: Surveys peer-to-peer + role-based coordination mechanisms covering CONDOLENCE design space.

independent_scoring.title: Handling Tool Errors and Agent Recovery (Langchain Production)
independent_scoring.rationale: Tool-level error handling + recovery patterns address the atomic restore aspect.

independent_scoring.title: Context Window like RAM not Storage (Mem0 2026)
independent_scoring.rationale: Cache invalidation + context pinning maps directly onto stages 1-2 (dry-eyes / open-ears).

independent_scoring.title: Why Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail (Redis)
independent_scoring.rationale: Handshake protocols + role-tagging cover restore-channel + role-anchor dimensions.

reason: All 8 independently scored results clear at least one hit criterion (8 keyword, 8 semantic >=0.7, 8 functional >=0.7). Total hits = 8 >> 1. Three core mechanisms (role-anchor registry, atomic 3-stage recovery, peer-driven multi-agent restore) are each independently covered by prior art (MAST, Mesh Memory Protocol, TraceFix, Galileo, Redis, Mem0). Recombination is not differentiated.
recommendation: Reject candidate. Recombination of (atomic recovery ritual + role-anchor registry + peer-execution) is fully covered by existing literature; cultural mnemonic framing does not introduce new technical mechanism.
